svg.text: The field always drifts. The cursor swirls, pushes, or pulls the points near it, and they settle back.

g.text: SWIRL — tangential 25 + inward 6 · PUSH — out 29 · ATTRACT — in 30
RETURN — offset decays exp: 1.2 normal, 0.35 while large (wake)
COMPUTE — cursor offsets on CPU, turbulence on GPU
KINEMATIC offset + decay — no mass, velocity, or collisions
1 — drift moves the whole field; the cursor affects only nearby points
2 — force is stronger near the cursor, zero past the radius
3 — swirl orbits the cursor with a slight inward pull
4 — displaced points settle back home, leaving a brief wake
5 — kinematic offset + decay, not a physics simulation

svg.text: MOUSE_RADIUS = 278 px · swirl 25 (+6) · push 29 · attract 30 · return decay 1.2 / 0.35.
A renderer — not a filter, not AI, not a physics simulation.
